text: The system must allow for multiple file upload to be configured on a per-page basis.
The system must allow actors to organize their voice clips into a portfolio.
The system must allow actors to download their voice clips in a flexible format.
The system must store audio clips in a format conducive to speech.
The system must allow actors to delete recorded clips.
The system must allow web feeds to be turned on or off on a per-page basis.
An actor must be able to search through pages in a course.
The system must display a search box on every page after an actor has logged in.
A course administrator must be able to grade an assignment.
The system should display grade information to the appropriate actor.
The system should maintain a grade history.
The system must provide a wiki where actors can collaboratively create networks of documents.
The system must provide a blog engine.
The system must provide both e-mail and SMS notifications for pages.
As far as possible, the system should provide a simple, responsive interface.
The system must be backed up on a configurable schedule.
The system must have the ability and capacity to restore back-up data within six hours so that the system is never offline for an inordinate period of time.
The system should be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
99% up-time should be considered sufficient to meet this requirement.
The system should support at least 1000 concurrent users.
The system must be maintainable without substantial modification.
Relevant, online documentation for users should be available on each page.
Users must have easy access to help while interacting with the system.
Adequate user documentation should be provided to minimize the number of calls to the Help Desk about problems with the system.
Modifications should be reported via the main page to inform actors of unexpected changes.
This electronic documentation should be supplemented with phone and on-site support provided by the Office of Information Services.
System administrators must have access to comprehensive, searchable documentation.
A comprehensive database of maintenance tasks and help files should be compiled to make the courseware system easier to maintain from an IT staff point-of-view.
Search results should be displayed based on relevance.
This documentation must cover all procedures necessary for regular maintenance with links to additional information, all common errors, and links or documentation for advanced troubleshooting.
The EIRENE network shall provide text message bearer service.
The EIRENE network shall provide bearer service for general data applications.
The EIRENE network shall provide bearer service for automatic fax.
The EIRENE network shall provide bearer service for train control applications.
The EIRENE network shall provide closed user group service.
The EIRENE network shall provide multi-level priority and pre-emption.
The EIRENE network shall provide advanced call handling, such as call hold, call transfer, call queuing, etc.
The EIRENE network shall provide auto answer service.
The EIRENE network shall provide barring incoming or outgoing calls.
The EIRENE network shall provide call supervisory indications.
The EIRENE network shall provide charging information.
The EIRENE network shall support functional addressing by train, engine or coach number or functional number.
The EIRENE network shall support call specific persons depending upon user location.
The EIRENE network shall support a specific mode for shunting operations providing a link assurance signal.
The EIRENE network shall support multiple driver communications within the same train.
The EIRENE network shall support railway operational emergency calls.
The EIRENE network shall provide a direct mode facility for local set-to-set operation without network infrastructure.
The EIRENE network shall provide set-up of urgent or frequent calls through single keystroke or similar.
The EIRENE network shall provide display of functional identity of calling/called party.
The EIRENE network shall provide fast and guaranteed call set-up.
The EIRENE network shall provide seamless communication support for train speeds up to 500 km/h.
The EIRENE network shall provide automatic and manual test modes with fault indications.
The EIRENE network shall provide control over mobile network selection.
The EIRENE network shall provide control over system configuration.
It shall be possible for applications external to EIRENE to access EIRENE bearer services.
All voice call services shall be able to operate between any combination of fixed and mobile equipment users.
The system shall support point-to-point voice calls between any two call parties.
Such point-to-point calls shall allow both parties to talk simultaneously.
The system shall allow a user to make public emergency point-to-point voice calls.
The system shall support broadcast voice calls.
The composition of call groups shall be able to be modified within the network.
A single user shall be able to be a member of one or more call groups.
The local area over which broadcast calls shall be implemented shall be able to be modified within the network.
It shall only be possible for the user who initiated the call to talk, other users can only listen (for broadcast calls).
The system shall support group voice calls.
The composition of call groups shall be able to be modified within the network (for group calls).
The local area over which group calls are implemented shall be able to be modified within the network (for group calls).
It shall be possible for controllers to speak at any time during a group call.
A mechanism shall be provided by the system to arbitrate between those users wishing to speak within the group call.
The system shall support multi-party voice communications between up to six different parties.
Any of the parties involved in a multi-party voice call shall be able to talk simultaneously.
The EIRENE network should support text messages.
The network should support the receipt of mobile-originated text messages by the ground.
If the text message facility is implemented, it shall not interfere with the ability of users to make or receive high priority voice or data calls.
The network shall support point-to-point data communications.
The network shall support data rates of at least 2.4 kbit/s.
The network should support fax transmissions between the ground and mobile users.
Where fax functionality is provided, it shall be possible to interrupt the fax to make or receive a high priority voice or data call.
Where ERTMS/ETCS level 2 or 3 is implemented, the network shall be capable of supporting data communications for that train control system.
The EIRENE network shall support display of identity of called/calling user.
The EIRENE network should support restriction of display of called/calling user.
The EIRENE network shall support priority and pre-emption.
The EIRENE network shall support closed user group.
The EIRENE network shall support call forwarding.
The EIRENE network shall support call hold.
The EIRENE network shall support call waiting.
The EIRENE network should support charging information.
The EIRENE network shall support call barring.
It shall be possible for the equipment to display the identity of the called or calling party in the form of a standard telephone number.
It shall be possible to display the identity of the called or calling party as a textual description of their function.
It should be possible for the network to prevent the identity of certain users from being displayed on the mobile, either when being called, calling or both.
The network shall provide a mechanism whereby calls may be assigned one of a number of different priority levels.
This mechanism shall allow calls with a higher assigned priority to override (pre-empt) existing calls of a lower priority.
Pre-empted calls will be discontinued and the new call of a higher priority shall be connected instead.
The group of users who may access the facilities of the EIRENE network shall be limited.
Any user who is not within the list of allowed EIRENE users shall not be able to gain access to any of the functions and services provided by the network.
It shall be possible for an incoming call or data message for one user to be forwarded to another user using functionality provided by the network.
In the case of voice calls, it shall be possible for the user who is attempting to forward a call to converse with the intended recipient prior to forwarding.
The network shall support automatically forwarding the incoming call without any user interaction (unconditional).
The network shall support automatically forwarding the incoming call without user interaction if the user is busy in an existing call (busy).
The network should support automatically forwarding the incoming call if there is no reply from the intended recipient (no reply).
The network should support automatically forwarding the incoming call if the intended recipient cannot be contacted via the network (not reachable).
The network shall allow the user to temporarily exit from an existing call by putting the call on hold.
It shall be possible for the user to re-join the call which is on hold at any time.
The network shall provide the ability to inform a user, who is involved in an existing call, of attempts by other users to contact them.
Where network services are chargeable, it should be possible for the network to provide information about call rates and on-going call charges.
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from making calls to another network (fixed or mobile).
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from making calls to certain types of numbers within or external to the network.
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from making calls to certain pre-defined telephone numbers.
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from receiving calls from all other networks (fixed or mobile).
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from receiving calls from certain other networks (fixed or mobile).
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from receiving calls from certain types of numbers within or external to the network.
It shall be possible, using network management or maintenance facilities, to prevent individual users from receiving calls from certain pre-defined telephone numbers.
The EIRENE network shall provide support for functional addressing including registration/deregistration (see section 11).
The EIRENE network shall provide support for location dependent addressing (see section 11).
The EIRENE network shall provide support for shunting mode (see section 14).
The EIRENE network shall provide support for Railway emergency calls (see section 13).
The level of coverage should be at least 95% of the time over 95% of the designated coverage area for a radio installed in a vehicle with an external antenna.
The network shall support all EIRENE-compliant mobiles.
The land-based part of the system shall provide communications for mobiles when stationary and when travelling at speeds up to the maximum allowable line speed or 500 km/h, whichever is the lower.
Railway emergency calls call set-up time shall be <2s*.
Group calls between drivers in the same area call set-up time shall be <5s.
All operational mobile-to-fixed calls not covered by the above call set-up time should be <5s.
All operational fixed-to-mobile calls not covered by the above call set-up time should be <7s.
All operational mobile-to-mobile calls not covered by the above call set-up time should be <10s.
All low priority calls call set-up time should be <10s.
The required call set-up times shall be achieved in 95% of cases.
Call set-up times for 99% of cases shall not be more than 1.5 times the required call set-up time.
Set-up times shall include the time required for any translation of functional numbers internal to the EIRENE network.

It shall be possible to determine the area over which a group or broadcast call takes place by one, or a combination, of the following: the location of the call initiator (if mobile-originated); the identity of the group being called (eg all users, all trains, etc); a prefix to the group identity specifying the call area (if fixed network-initiated).

Any group or broadcast calls initiated in a given location shall be broadcast over an associated area based on the location of the call originator, and also to any fixed network numbers associated with the originating location.
The definition of each broadcast or group call area should take into account factors such as the speed of trains on the line (stopping distance) and the operational control areas.
Mobiles configured for reception of railway emergency calls entering into a call area where a railway emergency call is ongoing shall automatically join this call.

Cab Radios configured for reception of a call to all drivers in the same area entering an area where a call to all drivers in the same area is ongoing shall automatically join this call unless involved in a higher priority call or involved in a call of the same priority.
It shall be possible to operate all mobiles in the frequency bands around 900 MHz, allocated for use by the railways, and in the public GSM networks.
Mobile equipment shall function correctly when travelling at speeds from 0 km/h to 500 km/h.
Cab radio shall support multi-party voice calls.
General purpose radio should support multi-party voice calls.
Operational radio should support multi-party voice calls.
Cab radio shall support general data applications.
General purpose radio should support general data applications.
Operational radio should support general data applications.
Cab radio should support automatic fax.
General purpose radio should support automatic fax.
Operational radio should support automatic fax.
Cab radio should support train control applications.
General purpose radio should support train control applications.
Operational radio should support train control applications.
Cab radio shall support EIRENE closed user group.
General purpose radio should support EIRENE closed user group.
Operational radio shall support EIRENE closed user group.
Cab radio shall support unconditional call forwarding.
General purpose radio should support unconditional call forwarding.
Operational radio should support unconditional call forwarding.
Cab radio should support "if user busy" call forwarding.
General purpose radio should support "if user busy" call forwarding.
Operational radio should support "if user busy" call forwarding.
Cab radio should support "if no reply" call forwarding.
General purpose radio should support "if no reply" call forwarding.
Operational radio should support "if no reply" call forwarding.
Cab radio should support "if not reachable" call forwarding.
General purpose radio should support "if not reachable" call forwarding.
Operational radio should support "if not reachable" call forwarding.
Cab radio shall support call hold.
General purpose radio should support call hold.
Operational radio should support call hold.
Cab radio should support display of call charging information.
General purpose radio should support display of call charging information.
Operational radio should support display of call charging information.
Cab radio shall support call barring.
General purpose radio should support call barring.
Operational radio shall support call barring.
Cab radio shall support auto answer service.
General purpose radio should support auto answer service.
Operational radio shall support auto answer service.
Cab radio shall support call supervisory information.
General purpose radio should support call supervisory information.
Operational radio should support call supervisory information.
Cab radio shall support location dependent addressing (section 11).
General purpose radio should support location dependent addressing (section 11).
Operational radio should support location dependent addressing (section 11).
Cab radio should support direct mode (section 15).
Operational radio should support direct mode (section 15).
Cab radio shall support shunting mode (section 14).
Operational radio should support shunting mode (section 14).
Cab radio shall support multiple driver communications within the same train (section 5).

Any environmental and physical requirements stated may be superseded by national requirements if the national standards provide a higher level of environmental and physical protection. However, stricter national standards shall not prevent the use of other EIRENE mobiles in that country.
All design, testing and installation of EIRENE mobile radio equipment shall comply with the quality procedures defined in ISO 90014.
Any pressure pulses caused by vehicles passing, entering or leaving tunnels shall not affect the normal operation of EIRENE mobile equipment.

The design of EIRENE mobiles shall comply with the relevant European Norms for protection against physical hazards, including pollution, chemical contaminants, flammable substances, biologically active substances, dust, sand and heavy particles, and mould growth.
All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be capable of being operated on board rolling stock.
The batteries to be used in the mobile equipment shall have sufficient capacity to support typical railway use.
A driver may be provided with a handheld portable to allow communications whilst the driver is outside the train.
Once a call has been established the connected parties shall be able to communicate.
Picking up the handset shall transfer the communication to the handset and reduce the loudspeaker volume to its minimum level.
Replacing the handset shall cancel the call.
Replacing the handset shall transfer the call to the loudspeaker at the initial volume.
A Push-To-Talk function shall be provided for use during group calls.
It shall be possible for the driver to initiate a call to the primary controller with a minimum of driver action.
It shall be possible for the driver to initiate a call to the secondary controller with a minimum of driver action.
It shall be possible for the driver to initiate a call to the power supply controller with a minimum of driver action.
Once an appropriate destination has been obtained, the radio shall attempt to establish a call to this destination.
The functional identity shall be displayed to the controller (upon call establishment).
The functional identity displayed shall include the train number, if available.
The functional identity displayed shall include the engine number, if no train number is available.
The functional identity displayed should include the coach number of the leading cab, if neither a train number nor an engine number is available.
An audible and visual indication shall be provided to the driver that the call is proceeding.
When the call is connected to the controller, an audible and visual indication is to be provided to the driver.
The call shall be connected to the loudspeaker at the pre-set value (when connected to the controller).
The functional identity of the connected party, if available, shall be displayed to the driver.
If the functional identity of the connected party contains an alphanumeric description, this shall also be displayed.
If the system is not able to connect the call, an audible and visual indication shall be provided to the driver that the call was not received by the controller.
The indication for a failed connection shall also indicate if the called party was busy or if the network could not connect the call.
It shall be possible for a driver to initiate and participate in group voice calls between drivers in a pre-defined geographical area.
A driver shall be able to initiate the establishment of a call to other drivers with a minimum of action (eg a single keystroke).
The group identity shall be displayed on the Cab radios of the participating drivers.
An audible and visual indication shall be provided to the driver that the call is proceeding (for calls to other drivers).
Once connected, the driver shall be able to communicate with other driver(s) by using the Push-To-Talk button on the handset.
An indication shall be provided to the driver as a reminder of the need to use the Push-To-Talk button on the handset if he wants to talk.
The call shall be connected to the loudspeaker until the driver picks up the handset (for calls to other drivers).
The call shall continue until terminated by the calling driver, an authorised controller or the network.
If the train moves out of the group call area whilst the call is in progress, an audible and visual indication of the loss of call shall be provided to the driver.
If the system is not able to connect the call, an audible and visual indication shall be provided to the driver (for calls to other drivers).
It shall be possible for a driver to initiate Railway emergency calls (see section 13).

If a train-borne recorder is connected to the Cab radio, details of the activation, termination and any failures of the emergency function shall be sent to the train-borne recorder as each event occurs.
A continuous visual and short audible indication (from 0 to 20 seconds, for trials: 5 seconds) that the emergency function has been activated shall be provided in the cab.
When the handset is off-hook, the audible indication (for emergency activation) shall be sounded from the loudspeaker at a low volume, but shall not be sounded from the handset.

Once the Railway emergency call is connected and the audible indication is finished, an indication shall be provided to the driver as a reminder of the need to use the Push-To-Talk button on the handset if he wants to speak.
The Railway emergency call shall be connected to the loudspeaker until the driver picks up the handset.

If the train moves out of the call area whilst the Railway emergency call is in progress, it will leave the call and an audible and visual indication of the loss of call shall be provided to the driver.
Once the Railway emergency call is terminated, the continuous visual alarm indication in the cab shall cease.
If the system is not able to connect the Railway emergency call, an audible and visual indication shall be provided to the driver.
It shall be possible for the lead driver to establish a permanent radio connection between each of the active cabs of multiple traction vehicles not connected by on-train wire connections.
Whilst an on-going multi-driver call, a ‘multi-drivers' indication shall be displayed permanently at all Cab radios.
The lead driver shall be notified if a member of the group has placed the multi-driver call on hold, although this shall not affect communications between the remaining members of the group.
At any time during a multi-driver call, the lead driver shall be able to remove a member of the group.
The lead driver shall be able to terminate the entire multi-driver call.
If a driver is disconnected from the multi-driver call, a clear indication shall be given.
The setting up and closing down of a multi-driver call shall be simplified using automation or guidance through the steps required.
In each cab, the multi-driver call shall be connected to the loudspeaker whilst the handset is on-hook.
If any part of a multi-driver call fails, an audible and visual indication shall be provided in the appropriate cab.
For calls between a controller and the lead cab, it shall be possible to add the controller to the multi-driver call.
If the controller calls the lead driver (in a controller-lead cab call), the controller is automatically added into the multi-driver call.
Functional identity of the controller shall be displayed in the leading cab (in a controller-lead cab call).
It shall be possible for the driver to contact members of on-board train staff using a point-to-point voice call.
Upon activation of the function to call train staff, the Cab radio shall provide the driver with a list of 'generic' staff, e.g. chief conductor, conductor 1, conductor 2, catering staff 1.
The driver shall then be prompted to select the train staff with which he wishes to communicate.

The Cab radio shall be integrated with internal communication systems such that the appropriate means of connection (wire or radio) will be determined automatically for staff connected by intercom or internal telephone.
The Cab radio shall be capable of being used as a standard telephone, such that the driver is able to call any valid number subject to pre-defined call restrictions.
The call from the Cab radio may be initiated by selection from a pre-defined list (up to 99 entries).
The call from the Cab radio may be initiated by direct dialling a subscriber number.
The call from the Cab radio may be initiated by calling a functional number.
An audible and visual indication of an incoming call shall be provided (for point-to-point voice call).
The functional identity (which may contain an alphanumeric description) of the calling party, when available, shall be displayed.
Point-to-point calls that are not automatically answered (see table 10-1) shall only be answered once the driver accepts the call.

Once the driver has accepted the standard point-to-point call, it shall be connected appropriately, e.g. if the driver has accepted the call by lifting the handset, then the call shall be routed to the handset.
An audible and visual indication of the incoming call shall be provided when a Cab radio receives a group or broadcast call.
The group identity of the voice group call (VBS or VGCS) shall be displayed.
Group or broadcast calls shall automatically be connected to the loudspeaker if the handset is not in use, or to the handset if the handset is off hook.
The driver shall be informed if a broadcast call is ongoing by a visual indication displayed on the MMI.
If the call is a group call, the driver shall be required to request permission before being able to speak as part of the call by lifting the handset and pushing the Push-To-Talk (PTT) button.
During a group call, a visual indication shall be displayed on the driver's MMI to remind the driver of the need to use the PTT button.
In a group call, an audible and visual indication of whether the request to talk was successful shall be provided.
The driver shall then be able to speak as part of the call whilst the PTT button is pressed.
If the train moves out of the group or broadcast call area whilst the call is in progress, an audible and visual indication of the loss of call shall be provided to the driver.
Reception of an incoming Railway emergency call shall proceed as for a standard group or broadcast call, except that a distinctive audible and visual indication shall be provided in the cab.
If a train-borne recorder is connected to the Cab radio, details of an incoming Railway emergency call shall be transmitted to the train-borne recorder for recording, see section 5.8.
An automatic confirmation shall be generated by the Cab radio at the end of the Railway emergency call as detailed in section 13.

The Cab radio shall provide a means for the driver to terminate calls which he is authorised to terminate (i.e. all calls except Railway emergency calls not initiated by the driver and shunting group calls).
For group or broadcast calls, a means to leave the call without terminating the call shall be provided.
The Cab radio system shall be able to receive incoming text messages (see section 12 for details).
The Cab radio shall support a ‘shunting mode' of operation that provides a link assurance tone to reassure users of the integrity of the communication link (see section 14).
A means to enter and leave shunting mode shall be provided.
The functionality to enter and leave shunting mode shall not be available when there are on-going calls involving the Cab radio.
The link assurance tone shall be provided via the loudspeaker.

It should be possible for the Cab radio to be used without the network service (if it should fail or if there is no authorised network coverage), such that local open channel communications are still possible (direct mode).
If direct mode is implemented, a means to enter and leave direct mode shall be provided.
If the radio is in direct mode and the normal network service returns, the user shall be provided with an audible and visual indication.
Where calls involve the Cab radio but not the driver, the driver shall be provided with an indication that the Cab radio is busy.
For voice calls using the Cab radio not involving the driver as a called or calling party, the driver shall have the possibility to listen to the audio output of the communication.
For voice communications, if the driver picks up the handset, the radio shall allow him to join the communication.
The driver shall be able to terminate communications (voice or data) by selecting another call.
Where the Cab radio is required by a national railway to support a handheld portable, the Cab radio shall provide a clear indication as to whether the handheld portable is activated or not.
Where the Cab radio is required by a national railway to support a handheld portable, the Cab radio shall provide an interface to allow the handheld portable to be recharged when not in use.
Where the Cab radio is required by a national railway to support a handheld portable, the Cab radio shall forward calls automatically to the handheld portable when the handheld portable is activated.
Where the Cab radio is required by a national railway to support a handheld portable, the Cab radio shall cancel any call forwarding to the handheld portable when it is deactivated.
Where the Cab radio is required by a national railway to support a handheld portable, the Cab radio shall allow the driver to override the call forwarding to the handheld portable.
Powering up the Cab radio will initiate automatic self-testing.
Powering up the Cab radio will initiate automatic selection of the pre-set loudspeaker volume.
Powering up the Cab radio will initiate registration with other on-train systems, such as ERTMS/ETCS.
All failures of self-tests should be recorded in the train-borne recorder.
Powering up the Cab radio will initiate connection of the Cab radio to an authorised mobile network, which shall be the network to which the mobile was last registered (where available).
If connection is successful, the name of the network shall be displayed on the MMI and an audible indication of successful connection shall be given.
If connection is not successful, an audible and visual indication shall be provided.
Powering down the Cab radio shall cause the disconnection of the Cab radio from the mobile network.
When switched off, the radio shall retain any numbers which are stored at the time the radio is switched off.
The MMI on/off control shall be designed to prevent accidental activation/deactivation.
Switching the MMI on shall cause a self test of MMI (e.g. transitory lighting of the display and of all the controls and indicator lights of the MMI).
Switching the MMI on shall cause determination of the status of the Cab radio, providing a display of radio status on the MMI.
When the MMI is switched on, if less than 't' minutes have elapsed since it was last powered down, the MMI shall power up with the same configuration.
When the MMI is switched on, if greater than 't' minutes have elapsed since it was last powered down, the MMI shall power up with default settings.

The time 't' should be able to be varied between 0 and 240 minutes, as a maintenance function, allowing the radio to power up in a consistent state following minor operational procedures or power interruptions.
Switch off shall be “soft" so that the Cab radio completes controlled termination of a current call before actually switching off.
Switch off shall be “soft" so that the Cab radio deregisters the train number (where applicable) before actually switching off.
Switch off shall be “soft" so that the Cab radio stores required data before actually switching off.
Switch off shall be “soft" so that the Cab radio confirms Railway emergency calls (see section 13) before actually switching off.
As far as possible, the soft switch off procedure should also apply on power failure.
When switched off, the public address function (if available) shall still be provided.
When switched off, the call Chief Conductor function (if available) shall still be provided.
When switched off, the intercom function (if available) shall still be provided.
It shall be possible to present radio related prompts and information in a number of different languages.
By default, prompts and information shall be displayed in the language selected by the train owner (as stored within the system).

It shall be possible for the user to display a list of available languages and select the language in which radio related prompts and information are displayed both at turn on and during a journey (e.g. where a change of drivers may occur).
The radio shall support at least ten different languages.
By default, the loudspeaker volume shall be set to a pre-determined level suitable for the operating environment (as stored within the system).
It shall be possible for the driver to increase and decrease the loudspeaker volume within the adjustment range selected.
When the handset is picked up, the loudspeaker shall continue to operate, but at a reduced volume level.
Using a simple MMI action, it shall be possible for the driver to view a prioritised list of all authorised mobile radio networks (see section 10.5).

When presented with this list, it shall be possible for the driver (using simple MMI actions) to select the required mobile radio network manually, whereupon the Cab radio shall attempt to attach to this selected network.
The manual network selection function shall not be available when there are on-going voice calls involving the Cab radio.
A visual confirmation of the new network identity shall be given to the driver when a manually initiated network change has been completed successfully.
The Cab radio should be capable of selecting the mobile radio network as directed by input from an external device (e.g. a balise).

If directed network selection is implemented and voice calls are ongoing at the time when the external device attempts to trigger a network change, an audible and visual indication shall be given to the driver and network change shall be deferred until the call is terminated or until coverage of the current network is lost.
If directed network selection is implemented, input from the external device(s) shall initiate the network change regardless of the identity of the current network and how it was selected.
The Cab radio should be capable of selecting the mobile radio network automatically from a list of suitable networks (see section 10.5).
If automatic network selection is implemented, it shall be possible for the driver to activate and deactivate automatic network selection.
Automatic network selection shall not be used for international border crossing.

If directed network selection and/or automatic network selection are implemented, the driver shall be informed by means of an audible and visual indication of the new network identity whenever a network change takes place without user intervention.
It shall be possible for the driver to register and deregister a train number by entering the train number.
It shall be possible for the driver to register and deregister a train number by initiating an automated request (applicable only for leading driver).
Where the driver is required to enter the train number information, the information entered shall be shown on the display and require confirmation by the driver before further actions are possible.
If an automated request is initiated (option 2), the driver shall be able to accept or reject the train number returned by the network.

Where ERTMS/ETCS and radio systems are available and the driver is required to enter the train number as part of the initialisation procedure, this task shall be carried out only once on the ERTMS/ETCS system and the entered number transmitted to the radio system.
The driver shall be warned (by audible and visual indications) if a train with the same train number is already registered on the same network.
A means to override the currently registered train number shall be provided to the “newly registering” driver.
The driver of the previously registered train shall be informed (by audible and visual indications) that his train number has been overridden by another driver.

Where a change of train number is required during the course of a train's journey, it shall be possible for the driver to initiate the change or override the automatic change, by entering the train number.
Non leading drivers shall be able to indicate to the system their location in the train during the registration procedure (2nd driver, 3rd driver etc).
The driver's functional number shall be automatically registered/deregistered when the train number is registered/deregistered.

The functional number of other on-train users who may be addressed through the Cab radio via the on-train bus shall be registered/deregistered automatically at the same time as the registration/deregistration of the driver's functional number.

Where an on-train bus is connected to the Cab radio, deregistration of functional numbers can be performed on an individual basis and also by the train driver, by one action for all users (including the driver), at the end of the journey when the train number is no longer associated with the train.
There shall be a fixed relationship between the telephone numbers of radios installed in the locomotive/coach and the stock number, which shall remain even if the radio is changed.
It shall be possible to register and deregister a stock number automatically using information from on-board systems.
It shall be possible to register and deregister a stock number automatically via a fixed interface between the locomotive/coach and the radio.
The Cab radio shall have a reconfigurable list of stored numbers that may be used to perform abbreviated dialling to named user identities.
It shall be possible for the driver to find and display stored numbers and their information.
It should be possible for the driver to store and overwrite numbers and their details in the Cab radio.
General MMI functions are required to support forwarding call to the driver handportable.
General MMI functions are required to support putting a call on hold.
General MMI functions are required to support displaying incoming call details whilst in an on-going call.
General MMI functions are required to support temporarily exiting an existing call to answer another incoming call.
General MMI functions are required to support multi-driver call service.
It shall be possible to initiate tests of the radio to provide the driver with a reasonable level of certainty that the radio and MMI are working.
Such tests shall not prevent calls.
The result of the tests shall be displayed in a similar format to the self-test when MMI is powered on.
For data applications, calls shall be possible even if the driver MMI is switched off.
The Cab radio shall provide a priority function for calls from different sources in order to handle contention for the radio.
Established calls shall be able to be either put on hold or cleared down (pre-empted) to meet other call priorities.
The Cab radio shall be capable of handling contention between calls of differing priorities.
An outgoing call request of higher priority than an established call shall take precedence over the established call.
Where pre-emption occurs, an advisory indication may be provided to the pre-empted parties.
Priority call handling facilities shall be provided such that the performance requirements defined in sections 3.4 and 10.2 can be met.
Upon the request of the driver, the Cab radio should be able to perform a suite of run-time diagnostic tests on all physical interfaces.
If run-time diagnostics are implemented, failure of an interface shall be reported to the driver via the display.
If run-time diagnostics are implemented, all failures should be available to be recorded in a train-borne recorder.
If run-time diagnostics are implemented, diagnostic tests shall not interfere with normal operation of the Cab radio.
Train-mounted equipment including the Cab radio terminal equipment, MMI and antenna shall comply with all specifications in section 4.3 and all of those defined in this section.

Cab radios should be able to continue to operate for a period of at least 1 hour in the event of failure of the train's power supply, based on 20% point-to-point calls, 5% group calls, and 75% standby.
The driver man-machine interface shall comprise a display.
The driver man-machine interface shall comprise a control panel.
The driver man-machine interface shall comprise a loudspeaker.
The driver man-machine interface shall comprise a handset with Push-To-Talk button.
Radio equipment installed in a driver's Cab shall not obstruct the driver's vision or otherwise hinder the safe driving of the train.
The design of the equipment shall make it possible to install it within a cab complying with the requirements of UIC fiche 651 concerning the layout of cab equipment5.
All call related functions shall be possible with the handset on or off the hook.
The driver shall be able to adjust the brightness of buttons, indicator lights and displays according to the ambient lighting in the cab.
The driver shall be able to adjust the contrast of the display.
The emergency call button shall be red and shall be protected against accidental use.
Any displays shall be clearly readable from a normal driver's position, assuming a normal reading distance.
Displayed characters shall have a minimum height of 5mm.
The following colours (red, green) shall only be used where mandated in 5.4.11.
The standard set of audible and visual indications specified in the CENELEC Standard for the Driver-Machine Interface for ERTMS shall be implemented in the Cab radio system.

Some Cab radios may need to be operated by staff wearing gloves and controls may need to be suitable for use in a wide range of conditions, e.g. splash proof and suitable for viewing in direct sunlight and in darkness.
Where there is a risk that stored numbers or other set-up details may be accidentally changed, facilities should be provided on the Cab radio in order to prevent this happening.
Cab radios shall provide a means of preventing tampering.
If contact with the mobile radio network is lost, then the Cab radio shall give an audible and visual indication.
A driver safety device (DSD) interface should be provided in traction units that are equipped with a DSD in order to support the transmission of a DSD alarm.
If a DSD interface is implemented, it shall be capable of being connected to a device complying with UIC fiche 6413 concerning automatic vigilance devices used in international traffic.
The activation of the driver safety device shall automatically trigger the Cab radio to send a data message.
The DSD alarm call/message shall provide the train number.
The DSD alarm call/message shall provide the engine number.
The DSD alarm call/message should provide location information.
DSD alarm information shall be transmitted to the primary controller.
Additional information (for DSD alarm) may also be provided if available from external systems.
The Cab radio shall be able to provide details of activation of Cab radio emergency call button to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
The Cab radio shall be able to provide details of receipt of incoming Railway emergency call to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
The Cab radio shall be able to provide details of termination of outgoing or incoming Railway emergency call to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
The Cab radio should be able to provide details of radio faults to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
The Cab radio shall be able to provide details of driver safety device alarm message transmission to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
The Cab radio shall be able to provide details of confirmation of Railway emergency call to a train-borne recorder or other equipment via a standardised interface.
On-train mobiles shall provide a standardised interface to ERTMS/ETCS.
Other interfaces may be provided in the Cab radio system.
The General purpose radio shall be capable of use as a standard telephone, such that the user is able to call any valid number subject to call restrictions enforced by the network.
The facility for a given General purpose radio to send a Railway emergency call (see section 13) shall be determined as a management function.
A special MMI action shall be required for a General purpose radio user to send a Railway emergency call.
It shall not be possible for any General purpose radio user to send a Railway emergency call by dialling a short code or telephone number.
An audible and visual indication of an incoming call shall be provided (General purpose radio).
Where available, the functional identity of the calling party shall be displayed (General purpose radio).
A short audible and visual indication of the incoming call shall be provided (General purpose radio).
The group identity of the voice group call (VBS or VGCS) shall be displayed (General purpose radio).
If the call is a broadcast call, the user shall be informed that he cannot speak as part of the broadcast call (General purpose radio).
If the call is a group call, the user has to request permission to speak during the call by using the Push-To-Talk (PTT) function (General purpose radio).
In a group call, a visual indication shall be provided to remind the user of the need to use the PTT function (General purpose radio).
In a group call, an audible and visual indication of whether the request to talk was successful shall be provided (General purpose radio).
The user may then talk whilst the PTT function is used (General purpose radio).
If the user moves out of the group or broadcast call area whilst the call is in progress, an audible and visual indication of the loss of call shall be provided to the user (General purpose radio).

Reception of an incoming Railway emergency call (if implemented) shall proceed as for a standard group or broadcast call except that a distinctive audible and visual indication shall be provided (General purpose radio).
The radio shall provide a means for the user to terminate calls which he is authorised to terminate (i.e. all calls except broadcast or group calls not initiated by the user) (General purpose radio).
The General purpose radio shall have an on/off switch.
Switching the General purpose radio 'on' shall initiate transitory lighting of any display, controls or indicator lights.
Switching the General purpose radio 'on' shall initiate automatic self-testing.
If the self-test fails, a visible and audible indication shall be provided (General purpose radio).
Switching the General purpose radio 'on' shall initiate connection to a mobile network.
If connection is successful, the name of the network shall be displayed and an audible confirmation of successful connection shall be given (General purpose radio).
If connection is not successful, an audible and visual indication shall be provided (General purpose radio).
Switching the General purpose radio off shall cause termination of all calls under the control of (i.e. initiated by) the radio.
Switching the General purpose radio off shall cause disconnection of the radio from the network.
Switching the General purpose radio off shall cause clearing the display and inhibition of functions.
When switched off, the General purpose radio shall retain any settings and numbers which are stored at the time the radio is switched off.
Prompts and messages shall be capable of being displayed in at least 10 languages (General purpose radio).
The user shall be able to select his preferred language for the display; this setting shall be retained by the General purpose radio.
When the General purpose radio is first powered on, the language that appears on the display shall be the default one set by the national railway.
Where more than one mobile radio network is available for use by a General purpose mobile, the selection of the network to use shall be automatic (see section 10.5).
When the General purpose mobile changes network automatically, a visual and audible indication shall be given.
It shall be possible for a General purpose user to override the automatic network selection manually.
The manual network selection function shall not be available during calls (General purpose radio).
It shall be possible to register a functional number initiated by the user and transmitted to the ground (General purpose radio).
The user shall enter the functional number which is transmitted to the network (General purpose radio).
Where a change of functional number is required, it shall be possible for the user to initiate the change (General purpose radio).
It shall be possible for the user to find and display stored numbers and their information (General purpose radio).
It shall be possible for the user to store and/or overwrite numbers and their details in the radio (General purpose radio).
A standard data interface should be provided to allow a computer to be connected to the General purpose radio.
It should be possible for a General purpose user to change the battery without the use of tools.
The weight of the General purpose radio including battery should not exceed 250g.

General purpose radios should be equipped with rechargeable batteries capable of providing a minimum of eight hours operation from a single charge based on 20% point-to-point calls, 5% group calls, and 75% standby.
Changing the battery should not result in losing data stored in the General purpose radio.
The General purpose radio should be suitable for use with a car adapter kit.
The General purpose radio MMI should comprise a display.
The General purpose radio MMI should comprise a control panel.
The General purpose radio MMI should comprise a loudspeaker.
The General purpose radio MMI should comprise a microphone.
The General purpose radio MMI should be suitable for use both by day and night.
The on/off control should be designed to prevent accidental activation/deactivation (General purpose radio).
Facilities should be provided to adjust the loudspeaker volume (General purpose radio).
A Push-To-Talk function shall be provided (General purpose radio).
The Push-To-Talk function does not need to be a dedicated button (General purpose radio).
Four buttons should be provided for designated stored numbers (General purpose radio).
Stored numbers and other set-up details should be protected against accidental alteration (General purpose radio).
If the ability to initiate Railway emergency calls is implemented in the General purpose radio, accidental initiation of a Railway emergency call shall be prevented.
A visual and audible indication shall be given when the remaining capacity of the battery is such as to enable only 10 minutes of full duplex call time (General purpose radio).
A network service availability indication shall be provided to the user (General purpose radio).
A visual indication shall be provided to the user if the network service is no longer available (General purpose radio).
An audible indication should be provided to the user if the network service is no longer available (General purpose radio).
The Operational radio shall be capable of use as a standard telephone, such that the user is able to call any valid number subject to call restrictions enforced by the network.
It shall be possible for the user of an Operational radio to establish a call with the primary controller with a minimum number of actions being required.
It shall be possible for the user of an Operational radio to establish a call with the secondary controller with a minimum number of actions being required.
It shall be possible for the user of an Operational radio to establish a call with the power supply controller with a minimum number of actions being required.
For some users (Operational radio), particularly for shunting team leaders, up to twenty valid numbers may be stored for one controller type.
For some users (Operational radio), the identities of the controllers corresponding to these valid numbers shall be displayed with an audible and visual prompt for one to be selected.
For some users (Operational radio), the number of the controller selected shall then be used to establish the call.
If no selection is made by the user within 10 seconds, the radio shall abandon the call attempt (Operational radio).
Once an appropriate number has been obtained (Operational radio), the radio shall attempt to establish a call to this number with railway operation priority.
An audible and visual indication shall be provided to the user that the call is proceeding (Operational radio).
When the call is connected to the controller, an audible and visual indication shall be provided to the user (Operational radio).
The functional identity of the connected party, if available, shall be displayed to the user (Operational radio).
Where available, an alphanumeric description of the identity of the connected party shall be displayed (Operational radio).
If the system is not able to connect the call, an audible and visual indication shall be provided to the user (Operational radio).
The Railway emergency call shall be able to be initiated using a single red button (Operational radio).
It should be possible, as a maintenance function, to enable/disable the emergency call function (Operational radio).
If the emergency call function is disabled, a visual indication and short audible indication shall be given when the radio is switched on (Operational radio).
If the emergency call function is disabled, a visual indication and short audible indication shall be given when the emergency button is pushed (Operational radio).
An audible and visual indication of an incoming call shall be provided (Operational radio).
Where available, the functional identity of the calling party shall be displayed (Operational radio).
A short audible and visual indication of the incoming call shall be provided (Operational radio).
The group identity of the voice group call (VBS or VGCS) shall be displayed (Operational radio).
If the call is a broadcast call, the user shall be informed that he cannot speak as part of the broadcast call (Operational radio).
If the call is a group call, the user has to request permission to speak during the call by using the Push-To-Talk (PTT) function (Operational radio).
In a group call, a visual indication shall be provided to remind the user of the need to use the PTT function (Operational radio).
In a group call, an audible and visual indication of whether the request was successful shall be provided (Operational radio).
The user may then talk whilst the PTT function is used (Operational radio).
If the user moves out of the group or broadcast call area whilst the call is in progress, an audible and visual indication of the loss of call shall be provided to the user (Operational radio).
Reception of an incoming Railway emergency call shall proceed as for a standard group or broadcast call except that a distinctive audible and visual indication shall be provided (Operational radio).
The radio shall provide a means for the user to terminate calls which he is authorised to terminate (i.e. all calls except broadcast or group calls not initiated by the user) (Operational radio).
The Operational radio should provide facilities to support shunting mode as described in section 14.
If shunting mode is implemented, a means to enter and leave shunting mode shall be provided (Operational radio).
If shunting mode is implemented, the functionality to enter or leave shunting mode shall not be available whilst there are on-going calls involving the Operational radio.
If shunting mode is implemented, the link assurance tone shall be provided via the loudspeaker (Operational radio).
A means to enter/leave the direct mode of operation should be provided (Operational radio).
The Operational radio shall have an on/off switch.
Switching the Operational radio 'on' shall initiate transitory lighting of any display, controls or indicator lights.
Switching the Operational radio 'on' shall initiate automatic self-testing.
If the self-test fails, a visible and audible indication shall be provided (Operational radio).
Switching the Operational radio 'on' shall initiate connection to a mobile network.
If connection is successful, the name of the network shall be displayed and an audible confirmation of successful connection shall be given (Operational radio).
If connection is not successful, an audible and visual indication shall be provided (Operational radio).
Switching the Operational radio off shall cause termination of all calls under the control of (i.e. initiated by) the radio.
Switching the Operational radio off shall cause disconnection of the radio from the network.
Switching the Operational radio off shall cause clearing the display and inhibition of functions.
When switched off, the Operational radio shall retain any settings and numbers which are stored at the time the radio is switched off.
Prompts and messages shall be capable of being displayed in at least 10 languages (Operational radio).
The user shall be able to select his preferred language for the display; this setting shall be retained by the Operational radio.
When the Operational radio is first powered on, the language that appears on the display shall be the default one set by the national railway.
Where more than one mobile radio network is available for use by an Operational mobile, the selection of the network to use shall be automatic (see section 10.5).
When the Operational mobile changes network automatically, a visual and audible indication shall be given.
It shall be possible for an Operational user to override the automatic network selection manually.
The manual network selection function shall not be available during calls (Operational radio).
It shall be possible to register a functional number initiated by the user and transmitted to the ground (Operational radio).
The user shall enter the functional number which is transmitted to the network (Operational radio).
Where a change of functional number is required, it shall be possible for the user to initiate the change (Operational radio).
It shall be possible for the user to find and display stored numbers and their information (Operational radio).
It shall be possible for the user to store and/or overwrite numbers and their details in the radio (Operational radio).
A standard data interface should be provided to allow a computer to be connected to the Operational radio.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to salt mist spray.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to torrential rain at up to 2mm/min.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to heavy hail.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to snow and sand storms.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to accretion of ice.
The Operational radio should be capable of withstanding exposure to corrosive atmospheres (including sulphur dioxide, hydrogen sulphide, nitrogen oxides, ozone, organic hydrocarbons).
The Operational radio shall be of a rugged design suitable for use in the railway environment by operational railway personnel.
The Operational radio should have a carry feature that allows the operator to instantly have both hands free without dropping the radio.
It should be possible to release the carry feature instantly in the event that the radio is caught on a projecting part of a moving vehicle.
Controls shall be designed such that they are suitable for use by people wearing gloves (Operational radio).
Controls shall be designed for use in a wide range of conditions, e.g. splash proof and suitable for viewing in direct sunlight and in darkness (Operational radio).
It should be possible for an Operational radio user to change the battery without the use of tools.
The weight of the Operational radio including battery should not exceed 800g.
It shall be possible to change the battery without losing data stored in the Operational radio.

Operational radios shall be equipped with rechargeable batteries capable of providing a minimum of eight hours operation from a single charge based on 20% point-to-point calls, 60% group calls, and 20% standby.
The Operational radio should be suitable for use with a car adapter kit.
The Operational radio MMI shall comprise a display.
The Operational radio MMI shall comprise a control panel.
The Operational radio MMI shall comprise a loudspeaker.
The Operational radio MMI shall comprise a microphone.
The Operational radio MMI shall be suitable for use by day and night.
The display shall be capable of displaying at least 6 lines, each of 20 characters (Operational radio).
Four buttons should be provided for designated stored numbers (Operational radio).
The emergency button shall be designed to avoid accidental use (Operational radio).
If shunting mode is implemented, the link assurance button shall be designed to avoid accidental use (Operational radio).
A dedicated Push-To-Talk button shall be provided (Operational radio).
A visual and audible alarm shall be given when the remaining capacity of the battery is such as to enable only 10 minutes of full duplex call time (Operational radio).
A visual indication shall be provided to the user if the network service is no longer available (Operational radio).
An audible indication should be provided to the user if the network service is no longer available (Operational radio).
The primary controller's MMI should queue all incoming calls or call requests.
The primary controller's MMI should display the queue to the controller, showing the functional identity and priority of callers.
High priority calls should be identified and presented at the top of the queue on the primary controller's MMI.
The primary controller's MMI should allow the controller to select any of the incoming calls currently queued by the system.
The primary controller's MMI should allow the controller to establish a call of Railway emergency, public emergency or railway operation priority to any mobile by selection from the display.
The primary controller's MMI should allow the controller to establish, close, enter and leave group calls (at Railway emergency, public emergency or railway operation priority).
The primary controller's MMI should allow sending and receiving of text messages.
For post incident analysis, the controller equipment should provide the possibility to record all operational speech and data calls.
It should be possible for calls to be answered automatically according to incoming call priority as defined in section 10.2.
The EIRENE system shall enable users to originate and receive calls by functional number.
Each mobile shall be identified by a unique telephone number.
Every on-train function shall be identified by a unique standard number.
Every on-engine/coach function shall be identified by a unique standard number.
Every shunting team number shall be based on an association of a service area identifier and a shunting team identifier.
Every maintenance team number shall be based on an association of a service area identifier.
Every maintenance team number shall be based on an association of a type of maintenance team (speciality code).
Every maintenance team number shall be based on an association of a maintenance team identifier.
Every controller number shall be based on an association of a controller location.
Every controller number shall be based on an association of a controller identifier.
The numbering for other teams shall be treated in the same way as maintenance teams in 9.2.4.2.
Group call service areas shall be freely configurable within the operational responsibility of each railway network.
Telephone numbers can be defined on a national basis, but codes for certain functions shall be used on an international basis in order to allow interoperability.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for routing calls to the most appropriate ERTMS/ETCS RBC.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for Railway emergency calls.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for routing calls to the primary controller.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for routing calls to the secondary controller.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for routing calls to the power supply controller.
Standardised telephone numbers shall be implemented for Public emergency calls.
In network boundary areas, the pre-defined local area shall be allocated on a bilateral basis.
Authorised users within the EIRENE network shall be able to receive calls from calling parties outside the EIRENE network.
Five levels of priority shall be defined: Railway emergency, control-command (safety), public emergency and group calls between drivers in the same area, railway operation, railway information.
In order to provide interoperability, priorities shall be allocated consistently across all EIRENE networks, as shown in the following table.
Railway emergency calls shall have automatic answering.
Railway emergency calls shall pre-empt Control-command (safety) and below.
Control-command (safety) calls shall have automatic answering.
Control-command (safety) calls shall pre-empt Public emergency, group calls between drivers in the same area and below.
Public emergency and group calls between drivers in the same area shall have automatic answering.
Public emergency and group calls between drivers in the same area shall pre-empt Railway operation, Control-command information and below.
Automatic answering for Railway operation (e.g. calls from or for drivers and controllers) and Control-command information calls shall be mandatory for Cab radio.
Automatic answering for Railway operation (e.g. calls from or for drivers and controllers) and Control-command information calls should be optional for other user equipment.
Railway operation calls shall pre-empt Railway information and below.
Railway information and all other calls shall not have automatic answering.
The lowest priority ongoing call shall be pre-empted before that of a higher priority.
Various types of call restriction may be employed by the railways as an additional security measure.
Any implementation of such call restrictions shall not affect international interoperability.
A mobile may be a member of a number of groups.
It shall be possible to ‘activate' or ‘deactivate' the mobile's subscription to these groups.
Activating a group on the mobile shall allow a user to receive a call from that group.
Deactivating a group on the mobile shall prevent a user receiving calls from that group.
Cab radios will be members of the Railway emergency call standard group.
Cab radios will be members of the 'all train drivers' standard group.
Cab radios will be members of the shunting team standard group.
Cab radios should be members of the trackside worker standard group.
All mobiles with Railway emergency group call subscription(s) shall be prevented from deactivating the emergency group(s) whilst operational.
Authorised networks shall be listed (or automatically selected if automatic selection has been enabled) in the following order: home EIRENE network, 'foreign' EIRENE networks, public networks.
Where EIRENE facilities are not available within the currently selected network, the user shall be given a visible indication.
The access matrix defines which subscribers are able to contact which other subscribers within the EIRENE network.
The network shall allow a call from a Primary Controller to a Lead Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Primary Controller to an Other Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Primary Controller to a Chief Conductor.
The network should allow a call from a Primary Controller to Public Address.
The network shall allow a call from a Secondary Controller to a Lead Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Secondary Controller to an Other Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Secondary Controller to a Chief Conductor.
The network should allow a call from a Secondary Controller to Public Address.
The network shall allow a call from a Power Controller to a Lead Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Power Controller to an Other Driver.
The network should allow a call from a Power Controller to a Chief Conductor.
The network should allow a call from a Power Controller to Public Address.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to a Primary Controller.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to a Secondary Controller.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to a Power Controller.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to an Other Driver, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to a Chief Conductor, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from a Lead Driver to Public Address, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to a Primary Controller.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to a Secondary Controller.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to a Power Controller.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to a Lead Driver, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to a Chief Conductor, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from an Other Driver to Public Address, at least for persons on the same train.
The network should allow a call from a Chief Conductor to a Primary Controller.
The network should allow a call from a Chief Conductor to a Secondary Controller.
The network should allow a call from a Chief Conductor to a Power Controller.
The network shall allow a call from a Chief Conductor to a Lead Driver, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from a Chief Conductor to an Other Driver, at least for persons on the same train.
The network shall allow a call from a Chief Conductor to Public Address, at least for persons on the same train.
If required, a railway may make additional restrictions to the access matrix.

An addressing scheme shall be provided which permits users to be identified by numbers corresponding to their functional roles rather than by numbers tied to the terminal equipment that they are using.
It shall be possible to assign up to a minimum of 3 functional numbers to an EIRENE user at any one time.
Only one EIRENE user shall be assigned to a given functional number at any one time.
A user shall be able to set up a functional number on one network, and cancel the number from another network.
The functional number shall remain valid as a user roams from one network to another.
The functional addressing scheme shall be independent of specific configurations of mobile and terminal equipment.
It shall be possible to call EIRENE users by functional numbers from a wide range of terminals (EIRENE and non-EIRENE).

The functional addressing scheme should permit calls to be routed from a controller to an international train within the control area without reference to any EIRENE system other than that providing service to the international train.
Functional numbers must be unique within the domain of operation.
Each functional number must be unique across all networks (including implementation of EIRENE facilities on public networks).
The functional number shall consist of numeric characters only.
The functional identity of the called user shall be presented to the user initiating a call and the functional identity of the initiator shall be presented to the user receiving a call.
For broadcast and group voice communications, the functional identity provided shall be that of the broadcast or group identity.
If the user initiating the call has more than one functional identity, the user shall be able to select, prior to call establishment, which functional identity is presented to the receiving user.

In the case of Cab radio, when a train number is assigned as a functional identity, this shall take priority over other Cab radio functional identities, and shall be the functional identity for the Cab radio to be displayed to other users.
The functional identity shall be presented to the user in a form which can be readily understood (e.g. 'driver of train abcd' rather than 'abcd01' or 'xyz shunting team 3' rather than 'xyz03′).
The functional addressing scheme shall be supported by a straightforward procedure for registration of functional numbers.
This procedure shall be carried out by the user on commencement of the functional role.
The functional number registration facility shall be supported by all EIRENE user equipment.
It should be possible for the system to prevent certain types of users from registering train numbers that they are not authorised to use.
It should be possible for the system to prevent certain types of users from registering driver of train functional numbers that they are not authorised to use.
It should be possible for the system to prevent certain types of users from registering shunting team leader functional numbers that they are not authorised to use.
It shall be possible to register up to ten functional numbers to items of equipment physically connected to the Cab radio within 30 seconds.
The registration procedure shall provide a means to prevent mis-allocation of functional numbers during registration.
Duplicate functional numbers (e.g. two trains with the same train number) shall be prevented.
The system shall provide a means to recover consistent data sets following a system failure during which functional addressing facilities are lost.
During the recovery period after a system failure, the system shall not permit the use of unverified functional numbers.
The functional addressing scheme shall be supported by a straightforward procedure for deregistration of functional numbers.
This procedure shall be carried out by the user at the end of the functional role.
The functional number deregistration facility shall be supported by all EIRENE user equipment.
It shall be possible to deregister up to ten functional numbers to items of equipment physically connected to the Cab radio within 30 seconds.
A given set of users shall be allowed to deregister a functional number which is no longer valid but which has not been deregistered by the user.
A given set of users shall be allowed to deregister, by overriding, another user of the same type (e.g. a driver can deregister a train number that another driver has forgotten to deregister).
A given set of users shall be allowed to deregister, with one action, all functional numbers associated with the same mobile (e.g. the driver deregisters all functions at the end of the journey).
An EIRENE mobile shall remove the displayed functional number and provide an indication to the user that deregistration has taken place.
To allow roaming between EIRENE networks, the system shall support a procedure for the re-registration of functional numbers after selection of a new network.
This re-registration procedure shall be carried out without manual intervention.
After automatic re-registration is performed, the new registration details shall be displayed to the user.
Location dependent addressing shall be provided to route calls for a given function to a destination number that is dependent upon the user's location.
Calls shall be routed based upon the location of the mobile for the Primary controller function.
Calls shall be routed based upon the location of the mobile for the Secondary controller function.
Calls shall be routed based upon the location of the mobile for the Power supply controller function.
Calls shall be routed based upon the location of the mobile for the Train management centre (e.g. RBC, CTS) function.
The correspondence between the locations and the destination of the call shall be easily re-configurable to support dynamic changes in controller area boundaries.
When operating with location dependent addressing, no manual action shall be required to update the system when a mobile moves between locations.
The location dependent addressing scheme shall be available to all mobiles.
As a minimum, the location information used by the EIRENE system shall be derived from that available from the network itself (e.g. current cell or base station serving the mobile).
Where greater accuracy for location dependent addressing is required, additional location information may be provided by ground-based signalling systems external to EIRENE.

Where greater accuracy for location dependent addressing is required, additional location information may be provided by on-train systems (e.g. ERTMS/ETCS equipment, balise readers, GPS etc) external to EIRENE.
It should be possible to transfer text messages between ground and mobile(s) through the EIRENE system.
In order to ensure interoperability, the text message service is mandatory for the Cab radio.
In order to ensure interoperability, the text message service is optional for the ground.
The maximum length of a message segment shall be 96 characters.
A message can include several segments.
The transfer time for each message segment should be less than 30 seconds for 95% of messages.
The text message facility shall not interfere with the ability of users to use the radio.
A standard data interface between the mobile and the application module shall be provided.
The type of Railway emergency call initiated shall be determined automatically, based upon the mode of operation of the radio.
If the mobile is in shunting mode, the emergency call button shall initiate a shunting emergency call.
If the mobile is not in shunting mode, the emergency call shall be a Train emergency call.
The Train emergency call shall be sent to all drivers and controller(s) within an area, which is pre-defined to meet operational requirements.
The predefined areas for emergency calls shall include, where necessary, parts of one or more network(s).
The appropriate ERTMS/ETCS RBC should be informed when a Train emergency call is initiated.
The Shunting emergency call shall be sent to all users involved in shunting operations in the shunting area.
The Shunting emergency call shall automatically take priority over the link assurance signal.
A Railway emergency call shall be able to be initiated by using a simple MMI action (e.g. a single MMI action for the Cab and Operational radios).
A connection of Railway emergency priority (see section 10.2) shall be established to a pre-determined set of receiving mobiles and controller(s).
If the system is not able to connect the call, the system shall automatically keep trying to connect the call for 30 seconds.
